title: Fix `test_log_completed_trial_skip_storage_access`
body:
Fix the first problem on #6206. `test_log_completed_trial_skip_storage_access` is incorrectly dependent on previously executed tests.

Reset the logger and set the logging level.

step_plan.summary: Update optuna/logging.py::_reset_library_root_logger to restore the library root logger's level to logging.INFO (instead of logging.NOTSET) when removing the default handler, ensuring tests and consumers start from a deterministic logging baseline and preventing order-dependent failures.

steps.operation: Restore library root logger level to INFO when resetting
steps.reason: The current reset helper removes the default handler but leaves the library root logger level at logging.NOTSET. Optuna's configured default level is INFO (set by _configure_library_root_logger); resetting should restore that default so tests and consumers see a deterministic initial logging state and do not become order-dependent.
steps.side_effects: Running this change will make _reset_library_root_logger set the logger level back to logging.INFO instead of logging.NOTSET. This should fix order-dependent tests (such as test_log_completed_trial_skip_storage_access) that rely on the library default being INFO. Potential impacts: any tests or third-party code that intentionally relied on the reset leaving the level as NOTSET will observe a different level (INFO) after reset and might need adjustment. No public API surface is added or changed, but consider adding a small public wrapper (e.g., reset_library_root_logger()) later to avoid tests referencing a private helper. After the change, run the full test suite to verify no other tests assume the previous NOTSET behavior; logging-related tests may need updates. There are no direct changes to storage or other runtime behavior, but downstream modules that check logger effective levels may see different values immediately after a reset.
